page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, and single-threaded gzip compression is in the bottom 10%. However, the 24 dedicated physical cores allow the instance to achieve average, middle-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also align with mid-tier performance, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g5.6xlarge offers a standard resource density for general-purpose workloads that benefit from parallel processing rather than high single-thread performance. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations, where the 24 dedicated cores can be fully utilized. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale language model inference, where prompt processing speeds for 7B models drop into the lower performance tiers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark evaluations reveal a distinct performance profile: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET throughput achieve average, mid-tier results. This resource distribution makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized workloads rather than single-threaded computations. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations that can leverage its 24 physical cores, whereas it is less optimal for single-threaded processing or intensive large language model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering mid-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 24-core general-purpose instance delivering balanced multi-threaded performance and 96 GB of memory for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Mid-tier multi-core performance contrasted with weaker single-core execution
Suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and Redis databases
